mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand that reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation signals strengthening economic output that supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during stable inflation regimes, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 3.5% year-over-year forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Markets absorb CPI readings above 3.5% without generating negative 20-day forward returns, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for prior rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue baselines, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for payroll growth falls below 0.02, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with monthly payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weakening final demand, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDP growth and retail sales declines fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting negative 20-day returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production gains combined with declining unemployment during a recovery phase signal robust capacity utilization that boosts near-term equity momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising industrial production and falling unemployment fails to generate positive 5-day returns in > 50% of recovery regime observations, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains negative.
